---
title: "Reading the Outbreak Report"
id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se02-sec03-sp01"
section_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se02-sec03"
session_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se02"
toc: True
---











<!-- IMPORT_START -->

Public health analysis must ultimately inform decisions. This requires clear and structured communication.

## Reviewing the Outbreak Report

<iframe src="../../resources/pdf/outbreak-report.pdf" title="Embedded PDF: View outbreak report" width="100%" height="600" loading="lazy"></iframe>

[View outbreak report](../../resources/pdf/outbreak-report.pdf)

## Accessing the Data

[Download dataset](../../resources/data/outbreak-dataset.zip)

## Communicating Findings

Effective reporting should:

- present clear results

- explain assumptions

- highlight limitations

- suggest actions

## Structuring Communication

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Clarity
Use simple and precise language\

## Evidence
Support claims with data\

## Action
Link findings to decisions

:::
## Final Reflection

::: {.callout-tip}
**Self-check:** Why is communication critical in outbreak response?

**Suggested answer**

Because decisions depend on how clearly findings are understood
:::

## Key Message

::: {.callout-important}
Evidence only has impact when it is clearly communicated and understood
:::

<!-- IMPORT_END -->
<!-- Standard workflow: content imported from Word -->
<!-- Advanced users may edit directly -->

<!-- START_NAVIGATION -->


```{=html}
<div class="custom-page-navigation">
    <div class="nav-prev">
        
        <a href="index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">← Previous</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Reporting and Action</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
    <div class="nav-next">
        
        <a href="OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se02-sec03-sp02.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">Next →</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Communicating Findings</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
</div>
```

<!-- END_NAVIGATION -->
